name: fortinet-fortirecon-aci
label: Fortinet FortiRecon ACI
description:
FortiRecon is a Digital Risk Protection Service (DRPS) product that provides an outside-the-network view to the risks posed to your enterprise.The Adversary Centric Intelligence (ACI) module leverages FortiGuard Threat Analysts to provide comprehensive coverage of dark web, open source, and technical threat intelligence, including threat actor insights. This information enables administrators to proactively assess risks, respond faster to incidents, better understand their attackers, and protect assets. This connector facilitates the automated operations related to ACI.

This connector has a dependency on the Threat Intel Management Solution Pack. Install the Solution Pack before enabling ingestion of Threat Feeds from this source.

fields.name: server_url
fields.title: Server URL
fields.description: Specify the URL or IP address of the FortiRecon server to connect and perform the automated operations.

fields.name: api_key
fields.title: API Key
fields.description: Specify the API key configured for your account for using the Fortinet FortiRecon ACI APIs.

fields.name: org_id
fields.title: Organization ID
fields.description: Specify the organization ID for fetch the records using the Fortinet FortiRecon ACI connector.

fields.name: verify_ssl
fields.title: Verify SSL
fields.description: Specifies whether the SSL certificate for the server is to be verified. By default, this option is set as True.

operations.title: Get Leaked Cards
operations.description: Retrieves a list of all leaked cards or specific leaked cards found for the organization ID specified in the configuration parameters and other input parameters you have specified from Fortinet FortiRecon ACI.

parameters.name: type
parameters.title: Type
parameters.tooltip: Filter by card type(s). Acceptable values are: DEBIT, CREDIT. Multiple types should be comma separated and will work as OR filter, example: DEBIT,CREDIT.
parameters.description: Specify the type of leaked card to retrieve from Fortinet FortiRecon ACI.

parameters.name: bin
parameters.title: Bin
parameters.tooltip: Filter by Bank Identification Number (BIN). Multiple BIN(s) should be comma separated and will work as OR filter, example: 123456,234567.
parameters.description: Specify the bin associated with the leaked card to retrieve from Fortinet FortiRecon ACI. For example, 123456,654321

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.title: Start Date
parameters.tooltip: Filter by leaked card's discovery date, default is last 6 months.
parameters.description: Specify the date from when to retrieve leaked cards from Fortinet FortiRecon ACI. By default, this is set to Now - 6 months (YYYY-MM-DD).

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.title: End Date
parameters.tooltip: Filter by leaked card's discovery date, default is today's date.
parameters.description: Specify the date till when to retrieve leaked cards from Fortinet FortiRecon ACI. By default, this is set to Current Date (YYYY-MM-DD).

parameters.name: page
parameters.title: Page
parameters.tooltip: Navigate between pages. Defaults to 1.
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify a page number to retrieve information from that page. Defaults to 1.

parameters.name: size
parameters.title: Size
parameters.placeholder: Must be greater 0 and less than or equal to 500.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the number of records to fetch per page. Default value is 10 and you can specify a maximum value of 500 per page.
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify the number of records to fetch per page. Default value is 10 and the maximum value is 500.

operations.title: Get Widgets
operations.description: Retrieves a list of all widgets or specific widgets for the organization ID specified in the configuration parameters and other input parameters you have specified from Fortinet FortiRecon ACI.

parameters.name: page

operations.description: Retrieves a list of all OSINT feeds or specific OSINT feeds for the organization ID specified in the configuration parameters and other input parameters you have specified from Fortinet FortiRecon ACI.

parameters.name: widget_id
parameters.title: Widget ID
parameters.tooltip: Specify the Widget ID using which you want to filter the OSINT feeds retrieved from Fortinet FortiRecon ACI.
parameters.description: Specify the Widget ID using which to filter the OSINT feeds retrieved from Fortinet FortiRecon ACI.

parameters.name: keyword
parameters.title: Keyword
parameters.tooltip: Specify the keyword using which you want to filter the OSINT feeds retrieved from Fortinet FortiRecon ACI.

operations.title: Get ICL Saved Searches
operations.description: Retrieves a list of intelligence collection lookup (ICL) searches saved for the organization ID specified in the configuration parameters based on the search keyword, query type, and other filter criteria that you have specified.

parameters.name: q
parameters.title: Search By Keyword
parameters.tooltip: Specify a keyword to search in saved searches by searching the given keyword in sources, query fields.
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify a keyword to filter the retrieve results by searching in sources and query fields of the saved searches.

parameters.name: alert
parameters.title: Alert
parameters.tooltip: Select 'True' to retrieve the list of saved searches that are enabled for notifications.
parameters.description: (Optional) Select True to return ICL saved searches that trigger alerts. Select False to return searches that do not trigger alerts.

parameters.name: query_type
parameters.title: Query Type
parameters.tooltip: Filter the results by specific query type. You can select between SYSTEM and USER.
parameters.description: (Optional) Select a query type to filter retrieved results. You can select from the following options: System User

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.title: Start Date
parameters.tooltip: Select the start date of the range containing the date when a saved search was added. Selecting a start date is mandatory if an End Date is selected.
parameters.description: (Optional) Select the start date of the range when a saved search was added. NOTE: Selecting a start date is mandatory if an end date is selected.

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.title: End Date
parameters.tooltip: Select the end date of the range containing the date when a saved search was added. Default is the current date.
parameters.description: (Optional) Select the end date of the range when a saved search was added. Default is current date. NOTE: Selecting a start date is mandatory if a start date is selected.

parameters.name: page
parameters.title: Page
parameters.tooltip: Navigate between pages. Defaults to 1.
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify a page number to retrieve information from that page. Defaults to 1.

parameters.name: size
parameters.title: Size
parameters.tooltip: Specify the number of records to fetch per page. Default value is 10 and you can specify a maximum value of 500 per page.
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify the number of records to fetch per page. Default value is 10 and the maximum value is 500.

operations.title: Get ICL Saved Searches By ID
operations.description: Retrieves a list of intelligence collection lookup (ICL) searches saved for the organization ID specified in the configuration parameters based on the search ID, time period, and other filter criteria that you have specified.

parameters.name: based_on
parameters.title: Get ICL Saved Searches Result Based On
parameters.tooltip: Select on what basis you want to fetch the ICL Saved Searches Result.
parameters.description: Select a criteria or platform based on which to fetch the ICL Saved Searches Result. You can choose from the following options: Archived Forums Defacements Forums Leak Docs Osint Feeds Pastes Ransomware Telegram

parameters.name: id
parameters.title: ID
parameters.tooltip: Specify the saved search ID to fetch its details.
parameters.description: Specify the saved search ID to fetch its details.

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.title: Start Date
parameters.tooltip: Select the start date of the range containing the date when a saved search was added. Default is last 12 months.
parameters.description: (Optional) Select the start date of the range when a saved search was added. Default is last 12 months. NOTE: Selecting a start date is mandatory if an end date is selected.

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.title: End Date
parameters.tooltip: Select the end date of the range containing the date when a saved search was added. Default is the current date.
parameters.description: (Optional) Select the end date of the range when a saved search was added. Default is current date. NOTE: Selecting a start date is mandatory if a start date is selected.

parameters.name: page
parameters.title: Page
parameters.tooltip: Navigate between pages. Defaults to 1.
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify a page number to retrieve information from that page. Defaults to 1.

parameters.name: size
parameters.title: Size
parameters.tooltip: Specify the number of records to fetch per page. Default value is 10 and you can specify a maximum value of 500 per page.
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify the number of records to fetch per page. Default value is 10 and the maximum value is 500.

operations.title: Get Stealers Infections Leaked Count
operations.description: Retrieves a count of systems for the organization ID specified in the configuration parameters that have been infected with credential stealers or malware programs designed to capture sensitive data, such as usernames, passwords, and other credentials from compromised machines; filtered by affiliated domain or stealer.

parameters.name: based_on
parameters.title: Based On
parameters.tooltip: Select on what basis to fetch the stealers infections leaked count. You can choose between Affiliated Domain, Status and Stealer.
parameters.description: Select the criteria based on which to fetch the count of systems infected with stealers malware and whose data might have been leaked in underground forums or marketplaces. You can choose from the following options: Affiliated Domain: Select to filter the count based on the domain or a set of domains associated with the stealer infections. Once selected, specify the following parameters: Page: Specify a page number to retrieve information from that page. Defaults to 1. Size: Specify the number of records to fetch per page. Default value is 10 and the maximum value is 500. Status: Select to filter the count based on the current status of the infections, such as whether they are still active, leaked, or resolved. Stealer: Select to filter the count based on the specific type of stealer malware involved. Once selected, specify the following parameters: Page: Specify a page number to retrieve information from that page. Defaults to 1. Size: Specify the number of records to fetch per page. Default value is 10 and the maximum value is 500.

Affiliated Domain.name: page
Affiliated Domain.title: Page
Affiliated Domain.tooltip: Specify a page number to retrieve information from that page. Defaults to 1.
Affiliated Domain.description: Specify a page number to retrieve information from that page. Defaults to 1.

Affiliated Domain.name: size
Affiliated Domain.title: Size
Affiliated Domain.tooltip: Specify the number of records to fetch per page. Default value is 10 and the maximum value is 500.
Affiliated Domain.description: Specify the number of records to fetch per page. Default value is 10 and the maximum value is 500.

Stealer.name: page
Stealer.title: Page
Stealer.tooltip: Specify a page number to retrieve information from that page. Defaults to 1.
Stealer.description: Specify a page number to retrieve information from that page. Defaults to 1.

Stealer.name: size
Stealer.title: Size
Stealer.tooltip: Specify the number of records to fetch per page. Default value is 10 and the maximum value is 500.
Stealer.description: Specify the number of records to fetch per page. Default value is 10 and the maximum value is 500.

operations.title: Get Leaked Stealers Infections
operations.description: Retrieves a list of systems for the organization ID specified in the configuration parameters that have been infected with credential stealers or malware programs designed to capture sensitive data, such as usernames, passwords, and other credentials from compromised machines; based on the search keyword, stealer name, and other filter criteria that you have specified.

parameters.name: q
parameters.title: Search By Keyword
parameters.tooltip: Specify a keyword to search in leaked stealers infections by searching the given keyword in username, stealer name.
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify a keyword to search in systems that have been infected by credential stealers by searching the given keyword in username and stealer name.

parameters.name: stealer_name
parameters.title: Stealer Name
parameters.tooltip: Filter the results by specific stealer name. Multiple stealer names should be comma separated and will work as OR filter, example: Vidar,Redline,Racoon,AZORult,risepro.
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify a keyword to search by the specified stealer name. Multiple stealer names can be specified as comma-separated OR values. For example: Vidar,Redline,Racoon,AZORult,risepro.

parameters.name: affiliated_domain
parameters.title: Affiliated Domain
parameters.tooltip: Filter the results by specific affiliated domain. Multiple affiliated domains should be comma separated and will work as OR filter, example: domain1.com,domain2.com
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify a keyword to search by the specified affiliated domain. Multiple affiliated domains can be specified as comma-separated OR values. For example: domain1.com,domain2.com.

parameters.name: status
parameters.title: Status
parameters.tooltip: Specify the status of leaked stealers infections, you can select between ACTIVE and RESOLVED.
parameters.description: (Optional) Select the current state of the stealer infection or the data leak event. You can choose from the following options: ACTIVE: Select to retrieve results where the infection or the data leak is still ongoing and has not been contained. RESOLVED: Select to retrieve results where the infection or the data leak has been contained or mitigated, and the risk from that specific incident has been addressed.

parameters.name: show_plaintext_password
parameters.title: Show plaintext password
parameters.tooltip: Select true if plaintext password needs to show in response. Default is false.
parameters.description: (Optional) Select true if plaintext password needs to show in response.

parameters.name: user_type
parameters.title: User Type
parameters.tooltip: Specify the user type of leaked stealers infections, you can select between EMPLOYEE and USER.
parameters.description: (Optional) Select the type of the user to filter based on the affected users who have had their credentials or other sensitive information compromised by stealer malware. You can choose from the following options: EMPLOYEE USER

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.title: Start Date
parameters.tooltip: Select the start date of the range containing the date when a leaked stealers infection was added. Selecting a start date is mandatory if an End Date is selected.
parameters.description: (Optional) Select the start date of the timeframe during which the data leak by the stealer infection was reported. NOTE: Selecting a start date is mandatory if an End Date is selected.

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.title: End Date
parameters.tooltip: Select the end date of the range containing the date when a leaked stealers infection was added. Default is the current date.
parameters.description: (Optional) Select the end date of the timeframe during which the data leak by the stealer infection was reported. Default is the current date.

parameters.name: page
parameters.title: Page
parameters.tooltip: Navigate between pages. Defaults to 1.
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify a page number to retrieve information from that page. Defaults to 1.

parameters.name: size
parameters.title: Size
parameters.tooltip: Specify the number of records to fetch per page. Default value is 10 and you can specify a maximum value of 500 per page.
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify the number of records to fetch per page. Default value is 10 and the maximum value is 500.

operations.title: Get Vendor Exposures By Vendor ID
operations.description: Retrieves detailed intelligence about vendor-specific security exposures or vulnerabilities tied to a particular vendor (a third-party supplier, software provider, or hardware manufacturer) vendor exposures for the organization ID specified in the configuration parameters based on the required information type and vendor ID that you have specified

parameters.name: type_of_info
parameters.title: Get Exposures For
parameters.tooltip: Select the type of information you want to fetch for vendor.
parameters.description: Select the exposure type to fetch for the specified vendor. You can choose from the following options: Attack Surface Exposure Darknet Exposure Incidents

parameters.name: id
parameters.title: ID
parameters.tooltip: Specify the vendor ID to fetch its details.
parameters.description: Specify the vendor ID to fetch its details related to the selected exposure type.

operations.title: Get Vendor Watchlist
operations.description: Retrieves potential security risks and exposures associated with specific vendors that are part of their supply chain or external partnerships for the organization ID specified in the configuration parameters based on the domain, name, and other filter criteria that you have specified.

parameters.name: domain
parameters.title: Filter By Domain
parameters.tooltip: Specify a domain to filter the vendors added for monitoring by domain.
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify a domain to filter the retrieved vendors added for monitoring by the specified domain.

parameters.name: name
parameters.title: Filter By Name
parameters.tooltip: Specify a name to filter the vendors added for monitoring by name.
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify a name to filter the retrieved vendors added for monitoring by the specified name.

parameters.name: approval_status
parameters.title: Approval Status
parameters.tooltip: Specify the approval status of the vendor, you can select between pending, approved, rejected.
parameters.description: (Optional) Select the approval status of the vendor. You can choose from the following options: Pending Approved Rejected

parameters.name: status
parameters.title: Status
parameters.tooltip: Specify the monitoring status of the vendor, you can select between pending, started, failed, completed.
parameters.description: (Optional) Select the monitoring status of the vendor. You can choose from the following options: Pending Started Failed Completed

parameters.name: page
parameters.title: Page
parameters.tooltip: Navigate between pages. Defaults to 1.
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify a page number to retrieve information from that page. Defaults to 1.

parameters.name: size
parameters.title: Size
parameters.tooltip: Specify the number of records to fetch per page. Default value is 10 and you can specify a maximum value of 500 per page.
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify the number of records to fetch per page. Default value is 10 and the maximum value is 500.

operations.title: Get Vendor Details By ID
operations.description: Retrieves a vendor's details for the organization ID specified in the configuration parameters based on the vendor ID that you have specified.

parameters.name: id
parameters.title: ID
parameters.tooltip: Specify the vendor ID to fetch its details. Get the vendor ID from the action: Get Vendor Watchlist
parameters.description: Specify the vendor ID to fetch its details like Alexa rank, approval status, and country..

operations.title: Get Vulnerability Intelligence CVEs
operations.description: Retrieves a list of Vulnerability Intelligence CVEs for the organization ID specified in the configuration parameters based on the source type, FortiRecon severity, and other filter criteria that you have specified.

parameters.name: input_src_type
parameters.title: Input Source Type
parameters.tooltip: Select the input source type based on which you want to filter the result. You can select between client and aci.
parameters.description: Select the input source type based on which to filter the retrieved results. You can choose from the following options: CLIENT ACI

parameters.name: recon_severity
parameters.title: Fortirecon Severity
parameters.tooltip: Select the severity based on which you want to filter the result. You can select between CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM and LOW.
parameters.description: (Optional) Select the severity based on which you want to filter the result. You can choose from the following options: CRITICAL HIGH MEDIUM LOW

parameters.name: nvd_severity
parameters.title: NVD Severity
parameters.tooltip: Select the severity based on which you want to filter the result. You can select between CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM and LOW.
parameters.description: (Optional) Select the severity based on which you want to filter the result. You can choose from the following options: CRITICAL HIGH MEDIUM LOW

parameters.name: years
parameters.title: CVE Year
parameters.tooltip: Filter the results by specific CVE year. Multiple CVE years should be comma separated and will work as OR filter, example: 2018,2019.
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify the year when the CVE was reported to filter the results by the specified CVE year. Multiple CVE years can be specified as comma-separated OR values. For example: 2018,2019.

parameters.name: input_src_subtype
parameters.title: Addition
parameters.tooltip: Select the addition type based on which you want to filter the result. You can select between EASM and MANUAL.
parameters.description: (Optional) Select the input source type to filter the results when the value selected in the parameter Input Type is CLIENT. You can choose from the following options: EASM IASM MANUAL

parameters.name: vuln_exploitation
parameters.title: Vulnerability Exploitation
parameters.tooltip: Select the Vulnerability Exploitation type based on which you want to filter the result. You can select between APT_GROUP, RANSOMWARE and UNKNOWN.
parameters.description: (Optional) Select the vulnerability exploitation type to filter the retrieved results. You can choose from the following options: APT_GROUP RANSOMWARE UNKNOWN

parameters.name: vendors
parameters.title: Vendors
parameters.tooltip: Filter the results by specific vendor. Multiple vendors should be comma separated and will work as OR filter, example: debian,canonical,nodejs,openssl.
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify the vendor based on which to filter the retrieved results. Multiple vendors can be specified as comma-separated OR values. For example: debian,canonical,nodejs,openssl.

parameters.name: products
parameters.title: Products
parameters.tooltip: Filter the results by specific product. Multiple products should be comma separated and will work as OR filter, example: debian_linux,ubuntu_linux,openssl.
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify the products based on which to filter the retrieved results. Multiple products can be specified as comma-separated OR values. For example: debian,canonical,nodejs,openssl.

parameters.name: tags
parameters.title: Tag IDs
parameters.tooltip: Filter the results by specific tag ID. Multiple tag IDs should be comma separated and will work as OR filter, example: 65dd1123aa1d5e9ab668ac56,56dd1768aa1d5o9ab668ac56..
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify the tag IDs based on which to filter the retrieved results. Multiple tag IDs can be specified as comma-separated OR values. For example: 65dd1123aa1d5e9ab668ac56,56dd1768aa1d5o9ab668ac56.

parameters.name: keyword
parameters.title: Keyword
parameters.tooltip: Filter the results by specific keyword. Provided keyword will be partially searched in these fields: cve id, . Example: CVE-2018.
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify the keyword based on which to filter the retrieved results. Specified keyword is used to perform partial search in the CVE ID field. For example: CVE-2018.

parameters.name: is_elevated
parameters.title: Elevated
parameters.tooltip: Select 'True' to fetch elevated CVEs.
parameters.description: (Optional) Select to fetch the CVEs (Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures) associated with a higher level of impact or severity.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.title: Sort By Recon Score
parameters.tooltip: Select the sort by type based on which you want to sort the result. You can select between recon_score:desc or recon_score:asc.
parameters.description: (Optional) Select the order by which to sort the results. You can choose from the following options: Sort by Recon Score Descending Order Sort by Recon Score Ascending Order
options: Sort By Recon Score Descending Order
Sort By Recon Score Ascending Order

parameters.name: data_sources
parameters.title: Data Sources
parameters.tooltip: Filter the results by specific data source.
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify the data sources based on which to filter the retrieved results. Multiple data sources can be specified as comma-separated OR values. You can choose more than one from the following options: ACI Reporting Darknet Chatter Working Exploits POC Exploits Security Blogs Hacktivist Chatter Social Media FortiGuard Outbreak Alert

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.title: Start Date
parameters.tooltip: Select the start date of the range containing the date when a vulnerability intelligence CVE was added.
parameters.description: (Optional) Select the start date of the timeframe during which the vulnerability intelligence CVE was added.

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.title: End Date
parameters.tooltip: Select the end date of the range containing the date when a vulnerability intelligence CVE was added.
parameters.description: (Optional) Select the end date of the timeframe during which the vulnerability intelligence CVE was added.

parameters.name: page
parameters.title: Page
parameters.tooltip: Navigate between pages. Defaults to 1.
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify a page number to retrieve information from that page. Defaults to 1.

parameters.name: size
parameters.title: Size
parameters.tooltip: Specify the number of records to fetch per page. Default value is 10 and you can specify a maximum value of 500 per page.
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify the number of records to fetch per page. Default value is 10 and the maximum value is 500.

parameters.name: size
parameters.title: Size
parameters.tooltip: Specify the number of records to fetch per page. Default value is 10 and you can specify a maximum value of 500 per page.
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify the number of records to fetch per page. Default value is 10 and the maximum value is 500.

operations.title: Get Stealers Infections On Sale Count
operations.description: Retrieves the count of compromised systems affiliated with the organization ID specified in the configuration parameters that are currently being offered for sale on darknet marketplaces.

parameters.name: based_on
parameters.title: Based On
parameters.tooltip: Select on what basis to fetch the stealers infections on sale count. You can choose between Affiliated Domain and Stealer.
parameters.description: Select the criteria based on which to fetch the count of systems infected with stealers malware and whose data is on sale in underground forums or marketplaces. You can choose from the following options: Matched Domain: Select to filter the count based on the domain or a set of domains associated with the stealer infections. Once selected, specify the following parameters: Page: Specify a page number to retrieve information from that page. Defaults to 1. Size: Specify the number of records to fetch per page. Default value is 10 and the maximum value is 500. Stealer: Select to filter the count based on the specific type of stealer malware involved. Once selected, specify the following parameters: Page: Specify a page number to retrieve information from that page. Defaults to 1. Size: Specify the number of records to fetch per page. Default value is 10 and the maximum value is 500.

Matched Domain.name: page
Matched Domain.title: Page
Matched Domain.tooltip: Specify a page number to retrieve information from that page. Defaults to 1.
Matched Domain.description: Specify a page number to retrieve information from that page. Defaults to 1.

Matched Domain.name: size
Matched Domain.title: Size
Matched Domain.tooltip: Specify the number of records to fetch per page. Default value is 10 and the maximum value is 500.
Matched Domain.description: Specify the number of records to fetch per page. Default value is 10 and the maximum value is 500.

Stealer.name: page
Stealer.title: Page
Stealer.tooltip: Specify a page number to retrieve information from that page. Defaults to 1.

operations.title: Get Stealers Infections On Sale
operations.description: Retrieves information on compromised systems affiliated with the organization ID specified in the configuration parameters that are currently being offered for sale on darknet marketplaces, based on searched keyword, stealer name and other filter criteria that you have specified.

parameters.name: search_string
parameters.title: Search By Keyword
parameters.tooltip: Specify a keyword to search in stealers infections on sale by searching the given keyword in stealer name, sites, country, isp, state.
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify a keyword to search in systems that have been infected by credential stealers by searching the given keyword in username, stealer name, sites, country, ISP, and state fields.

parameters.name: stealers
parameters.title: Stealer Name
parameters.tooltip: Filter the results by specific stealer name. Multiple stealer names should be comma separated and will work as OR filter, example: Vidar,Redline,Racoon,AZORult,risepro.
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify a keyword to filter the results by the specified stealer name. Multiple stealer names can be specified as comma-separated OR values. For example: Vidar,Redline,Racoon,AZORult,risepro.

parameters.name: marketplaces
parameters.title: Marketplace Name
parameters.tooltip: Filter the results by specific marketplace name. Multiple marketplace names should be comma separated and will work as OR filter, example: 2easy,genesis,russian market.
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify a marketplace name to filter the results by the specified marketplace name. Multiple marketplace names can be specified as comma-separated OR values. For example: 2easy,genesis,russian market.

parameters.name: isps
parameters.title: ISP Name
parameters.tooltip: Filter the results by specific ISP name. Multiple ISP names should be comma separated and will work as OR filter, example: Amazon.com,IRANET TELECOM.
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify a ISP name to filter the results by the specified ISP. Multiple ISPs can be specified as comma-separated OR values. For example: Amazon.com,IRANET TELECOM

parameters.name: countries
parameters.title: Country Name
parameters.tooltip: Filter the results by specific country name. Multiple country names should be comma separated and will work as OR filter, example: India,Colombia.
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify a country name to filter the results by the specified country. Multiple countries can be specified as comma-separated OR values. For example: India,Colombia.

parameters.name: states
parameters.title: State Name
parameters.tooltip: Filter the results by specific State name. Multiple State names should be comma separated and will work as OR filter, example: India,Colombia.
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify a state name to filter the results by the specified state. Multiple states can be specified as comma-separated OR values. For example: Indiana,Texas.

parameters.name: matched_domains
parameters.title: Matched Domain
parameters.tooltip: Filter the results by specific matched domain. Multiple matched domains should be comma separated and will work as OR filter, example: domain1.com,domain2.com
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify a keyword to search by the specified matched domain. Multiple matched domains can be specified as comma-separated OR values. For example: domain1.com,domain2.com.

parameters.name: status
parameters.title: Status
parameters.tooltip: Specify the status of stealers infections on sale, you can select between ACTIVE and RESOLVED.
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify the status of stolen credentials that are on sale. You can choose from the following options: ACTIVE RESOLVED

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.title: Start Date
parameters.tooltip: Select the start date of the range containing the date when a stealers infection on sale was added. Selecting a start date is mandatory if an End Date is selected.
parameters.description: (Optional) Select the start date of the timeframe during which the leaked credentials appearing on marketplace was reported. NOTE: Selecting a start date is mandatory if an End Date is selected.

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.title: End Date
parameters.tooltip: Select the end date of the range containing the date when a stealers infection on sale was added. Default is the current date and start_date is mandatory parameter if using end_date.
parameters.description: (Optional) Select the end date of the timeframe during which the leaked credentials appearing on marketplace was reported. Default is the current date.

parameters.name: page
parameters.title: Page
parameters.tooltip: Navigate between pages. Defaults to 1.
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify a page number to retrieve information from that page. Defaults to 1.

parameters.name: size
parameters.title: Size
parameters.tooltip: Specify the number of records to fetch per page. Default value is 10 and you can specify a maximum value of 500 per page.
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify the number of records to fetch per page. Default value is 10 and the maximum value is 500.

operations.title: Get Ransomware Victims
operations.description: Retrieves a list of ransomware victims affiliated with the organization ID specified in the configuration parameters based on the searched keyword, ransomware name, and other filter criteria that you have specified.

parameters.name: q
parameters.title: Search By Keyword
parameters.tooltip: Specify a keyword to search in ransomware victims by searching the given keyword in title, ransomware name, description fields.
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify the keyword based on which to filter the retrieved results. Specified keyword is used to perform search in the title, ransomware name, and description fields.

parameters.name: ransomware_name
parameters.title: Filter By Ransomware Name
parameters.tooltip: Specify a ransomware name to filter the ransomware victims by ransomware name.
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify a ransomware name based on which to filter the ransomware victims.

parameters.name: country
parameters.title: Country
parameters.tooltip: Filter the ransomware victims by specific country/countries. Multiple countries should be comma separated and will work as OR filter, example: India, United States.
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify a country name to filter the results by the specified country. Multiple countries can be specified as comma-separated OR values. For example: India,Colombia.

parameters.name: sectors
parameters.title: Sectors
parameters.tooltip: Filter the ransomware victims by specific sector(s). Multiple sectors should be comma separated and will work as OR filter, example: Business Services,Accounting Services.
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify a sector name to filter the results by the specified sector. Multiple sectors can be specified as comma-separated OR values. For example: Business Services,Accounting Services.

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.title: Start Date
parameters.tooltip: Specify the date from when you want to retrieve ransomware victims.
parameters.description: (Optional) Select the start date of the timeframe during which to retrieve ransomware victims.

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.title: End Date
parameters.tooltip: Specify the date till when you want to retrieve ransomware victims.
parameters.description: (Optional) Select the end date of the timeframe during which to retrieve ransomware victims.

parameters.name: page
parameters.title: Page
parameters.tooltip: Navigate between pages. Defaults to 1.
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify a page number to retrieve information from that page. Defaults to 1.

parameters.name: size
parameters.title: Size
parameters.tooltip: Specify the number of records to fetch per page. Default value is 10 and you can specify a maximum value of 500 per page.
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify the number of records to fetch per page. Default value is 10 and the maximum value is 500.

parameters.name: id

parameters.tooltip: Specify the ransomware victim ID to fetch its details.
parameters.description: Specify the ransomware victim ID to fetch its details.

operations.title: Get Ransomware Intelligence Stats

parameters.name: type_of_info
parameters.title: Get Ransomware Intelligence Stats For

You can choose from the following options: Top Ransomware Victims By Sector Top Ransomware Victims By Revenue Top Ransomware Victims By Country Top Ransomware Groups Ransomware Victims Over Time Latest Active Ransomware Groups

parameters.name: time_range_type

parameters.description: (Optional) Specify the time range to get the ransomware intelligence stats. You can choose from the following options: All Time Last 90 Days Last 30 Days Last 15 Days Last 7 Days Current Month Previous Month

parameters.description: (Optional) Select the start date of the timeframe during which to retrieve intelligence stats.

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.title: End Date
parameters.tooltip: Specify the date till when you want to retrieve ransomware intelligence stats.
parameters.description: (Optional) Select the end date of the timeframe during which to retrieve intelligence stats.

operations.title: Get Ransomware Threat Campaign

parameters.description: (Optional) Specify an actor name to filter the results by the specified actor. Multiple actors can be specified as comma-separated OR values. For example: abc,pqr.

parameters.name: sectors

parameters.description: Specify the ransomware Group Name to fetch its information.

operations.title: Update Stealers Leaked Status
operations.description: Updates the status of stealer malware that has been leaked or exposed, typically in underground forums or marketplaces based on the stealers leaked record ID.

parameters.name: stealers_leaked_id
parameters.title: Stealers Leaked Record ID
parameters.description: Specify the stealers leaked record ID to update the status.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the Stealers Leaked Record ID to update the status.

parameters.title: Status
parameters.name: status
parameters.tooltip: Specify the status to update the Stealers Leaked Record.
parameters.description: Specify the status to update to the Stealers Leaked Record. You can choose from: ACTIVE RESOLVED

operations.title: Update Stealers On Sale(Marketplaces) Status
operations.description: Updates the status of stealer malware that is currently being sold or traded on underground marketplaces based on the stealers record ID.

parameters.name: stealers_on_sale_id
parameters.title: Stealers On Sale(Marketplaces) Record ID
parameters.description: Specify the stealers on sale (marketplaces) record ID to update the status.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the Stealers On Sale(Marketplaces) Record ID to update the status.

parameters.title: Status
parameters.name: status
parameters.tooltip: Specify the status to update the Stealers On Sale(Marketplaces) Record. You can choose from ACTIVE, RESOLVED.

operations.title: Get Adversary Centric Intelligence ACI Reports
operations.description: Retrieves ACI reports for the organization ID specified in the configuration parameters published by Fortinet FortiRecon ACI, based on the provided input parameters.

parameters.name: report_id
parameters.title: Report Id
parameters.tooltip: Filter by specific report id(s). Multiple report id's should be comma separated and will work as OR filter.
parameters.description: Specify a single report ID or a comma-separated list of report IDs to be retrieved from FortiRecon ACI.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.title: Tag
parameters.tooltip: Filter by specific tag(s). Multiple tags should be comma separated and will work as OR filter, example: phishing,malware,exploited.
parameters.description: Specify a single tag or a comma-separated list of tags associated with the reports to be retrieved from FortiRecon ACI. For example,Data Breach,Cyber Crime.

parameters.name: adversaries
parameters.title: Adversaries
parameters.tooltip: Filter by specific adversary (actor names, APT groups). Multiple adversary should be comma separated and will work as OR filter, example: apt35,foo,bar.
parameters.description: Specify a single adversary name or a comma-separated list of adversary names associated with the reports to be retrieved from FortiRecon ACI. For example,Foo,APT 34.

parameters.name: source
parameters.title: Source
parameters.tooltip: Filter by specific source. Example: ,Exploit Forum,TECHINT, FortiGuard Threat Research, FortiGuard Research.
parameters.description: Specify a single source or a comma-separated list of sources associated with the reports to be retrieved from FortiRecon ACI. The comma-separation works as an OR filter, for example:FortiGuard Research,Exploit Forum,TECHINT

parameters.name: source_category
parameters.title: Source Category
parameters.tooltip: Filter by specific source category. Acceptable values are: `Media Trends`, `Darknet`, `Humint`, `OSINT`, `Fraudulent Domains`, `Technical Intelligence`, `Executive Summary`.
parameters.description: Specify a single source category or a comma-separated list of source categories associated with the reports to be retrieved from FortiRecon ACI. For example, OSINT,Darknet.

parameters.name: report_type
parameters.title: Report Type
parameters.tooltip: Filter by specific report type(s). Acceptable values are: `THREAT_ALERT`, `FLASH_REPORT`, `THREAT_REPORT`, `FLASH_ALERT`. Multiple report types should be comma separated and will work as OR filter, example: THREAT_ALERT,FLASH_REPORT.
parameters.description: Specify a single report type or a comma-separated list of report type associated with the reports to be retrieved from FortiRecon ACI. For example,THREAT_ALERT,FLASH_REPORT.

parameters.name: industries
parameters.title: Industries
parameters.tooltip: Filter reports by specific industry. Multiple industries should be comma separated and will work as OR filter, example: manufacturing,government,hospitality.
parameters.description: Specify a single industry or a comma-separated list of industries associated with the reports to be retrieved from FortiRecon ACI. For example,manufacturing,government,hospitality.

parameters.name: geographies
parameters.title: Geographies
parameters.tooltip: Filter reports by geography. Multiple geographies should be comma separated and will work as OR filter, example: south america,western europe, east asia,africa.
parameters.description: Specify a single geography or a comma-separated list of geographies associated with the reports to be retrieved from FortiRecon ACI. For example,Western Europe,South East Asia.

parameters.name: iocs
parameters.title: Iocs
parameters.tooltip: Filter reports by specific Ioc value or Iocs name. Available Iocs names are: IP, IPV6, URL, DOMAIN, EMAIL, CVE, SUB_DOMAIN, FILE_NAME, REGISTRY_KEY, HASH. Iocs values example: https://url.com, 12.11.20.111.
parameters.description: Specify a IOC value or IOC name to retrieve reports from FortiRecon ACI that are associated with the IOCs. Example: url.com, 12.11.20.111.

parameters.name: source_reliability
parameters.title: Source Reliability
parameters.tooltip: Filter by specific source reliability. Acceptable values are: `A - Reliable`, `B - Usually reliable`, `C - Fairly reliable`, `D - Not usually reliable`, `E - Unreliable`, `F - Cannot be judged`. Multiple source reliabilities should be comma separated and will work as OR filter, example: F - Cannot be judged,A - Reliable.
parameters.description: Specify a single source reliability or a comma-separated list of source reliability associated with the reports to be retrieved from FortiRecon ACI. For example: A - Reliable,B - Usually reliable.

parameters.name: information_reliability
parameters.title: Information Reliability
parameters.tooltip: Filter by specific information reliability. Acceptable values are:1 - Confirmed, 2 - Probably true, 3 - Possibly true, 4 - Doubtfully true, 5 - Improbable, 6 - Cannot be judged. Multiple information reliabilities should be comma separated and will work as OR filter, example: 1 - Confirmed,2 - Probably true.
parameters.description: Specify a single information reliability or a comma-separated list of information reliabilities associated with the reports to be retrieved from FortiRecon ACI. For example:4 - Doubtfully true,5 - Improbable.

parameters.name: report_generator_source
parameters.title: Report Generator Source
parameters.tooltip: Filter by specific report generation source. Acceptable values are:`AI INSIGHT`, `Analyst Curated Reports`, `Automated Reports`.
parameters.description: Specify a single report generation source or a comma-separated list of report generation sources associated with the reports to be retrieved from FortiRecon ACI. For example:AI INSIGHT,Automated Reports.

parameters.name: insight_relevance
parameters.title: Report insight relevance
parameters.tooltip: Filter by specific insight relevance. Acceptable values are: `MEDIUM`, `LOW`, `INFORMATIONAL`, `HIGH`.
parameters.description: Specify a single insight relevance or a comma-separated list of insight relevances associated with the reports to be retrieved from FortiRecon ACI. For examaple: HIGH,MEDIUM.

parameters.name: motivations
parameters.title: Motivations
parameters.tooltip: Filter by specific motivations. example: Hacktivist, Cyber Crime.
parameters.description: Specify a single motivation or a comma-separated list of motivations associated with the reports to be retrieved from FortiRecon ACI. For example: Hacktivist,Cyber Crime.

parameters.name: keyword
parameters.title: Keyword
parameters.tooltip: Filter by specific keyword. Provided keyword will be searched in these fields: Report ID, Report Title, Report Summary, Report Content, Source Category, Actors, Motivation, Threats, Industry Tags.
parameters.description: Specify a keyword to filter the reports retrieved from Fortinet FortiRecon ACI. The provided keyword will be searched in these fields: Report ID, Report Title, Report Summary, Report Content, Source Category, Actors, Motivation, Threats, Industry Tags.

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.title: Start Date
parameters.tooltip: Filter by report published date. Format: (YYYY-MM-DD, YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS).
parameters.description: Specify the date from when you want to retrieve reports from Fortinet FortiRecon ACI. Supported formats: YYYY-MM-DD, YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.title: End Date
parameters.tooltip: Filter by report published date, default is today's date. Start date is required to use this filter. Format: (YYYY-MM-DD, YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS).
parameters.description: Specify the date till when you want to retrieve reports from Fortinet FortiRecon ACI. By default, this is set to Current Date. Supported formats: YYYY-MM-DD, YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.

parameters.name: page
parameters.title: Page
parameters.tooltip: Navigate between pages. Defaults to 1. Maximum allowed records: page*size<10000. Apply parameter filters for specific results.
parameters.description: Specify a page number to retrieve information from that page.

parameters.name: size
parameters.title: Size
parameters.placeholder: Must be greater 0 and less than or equal to 500. Maximum allowed records: page*size<10000. Apply parameter filters for specific results.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the number of records to fetch per page. Default value is 10 and you can specify a maximum value of 500 per page.
parameters.description: Specify the number of records to fetch per page. Default value is 10 and the maximum value is 500. Value must be greater 0 and less than or equal to 500.

operations.title: Get Specific Adversary Centric Intelligence ACI Report
operations.description: Retrieves a specific ACI report for the organization ID specified in the configuration parameters published by Fortinet FortiRecon ACI, based on the provided ACI report ID.

parameters.name: report_id
parameters.title: ACI Report ID
parameters.description: Specify the ACI Intel Report ID to retreive details about the specific report from FortiRecon ACI.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the report ID to filter the IOCs of the specified report.

operations.title: Get Intel IOCs
operations.description: Retrieves Intel IOCs published in ACI for the organization ID specified in the configuration parameters published by Fortinet FortiRecon ACI, based on the provided input parameters.

parameters.name: name
parameters.title: IOC Name
parameters.tooltip: Filter by specific ACI IOC name.
parameters.description: Specify a ACI IOC name to filter IOCs to be retrieved from FortiRecon ACI.

parameters.name: report_ids
parameters.title: Report ID's
parameters.tooltip: Filter by specific ACI report ID(s). Multiple IDs should be comma separated and will work as OR filter, example: 2019091459522,2019090116811.
parameters.description: Specify a single report ID or a comma-separated list of report IDs to filter IOCs to be retrieved from FortiRecon ACI. The comma-separation works as an OR filter.

parameters.name: type
parameters.title: IOC Type
parameters.tooltip: Filter by specific Indicator of Compromise (IOC) type(s). Acceptable values are: IP, IPV6, URL, HASH, DOMAIN, SUB_DOMAIN, EMAIL, CVE, FILE_PATH, FILE_NAME, REGISTRY_KEY.
parameters.description: Specify a single IOC type or a comma-separated list of IOC types to filter IOCs to be retrieved from FortiRecon ACI. You can specfify the following values: IP,IPV6,URL,HASH,DOMAIN,,SUB_DOMAINEMAIL,CVE,FILE_PATH,FILE_NAMEREGISTRY_KEY.

parameters.name: first_seen
parameters.title: First seen
parameters.tooltip: Filter by Filter by first_seen date in this format: (YYYY-MM-DD).
parameters.description: Specify the first_seen date of IOCs you want to retrieve from Fortinet FortiRecon ACI. Supported format: YYYY-MM-DD.

parameters.name: last_seen
parameters.title: Last seen
parameters.tooltip: Filter by Filter by last_seen date in this format: (YYYY-MM-DD).
parameters.description: Specify the last_seen date of IOCs you want to retrieve from Fortinet FortiRecon ACI. Supported format: YYYY-MM-DD.

parameters.name: keyword
parameters.title: Keyword
parameters.tooltip: Filter by specific keyword. Provided keyword will be searched in these fields: name
parameters.description: Specify a keyword to filter the IOCs retrieved from Fortinet FortiRecon ACI. The provided keyword will be searched in these fields: name

parameters.name: sort
parameters.title: Sort
parameters.tooltip: Sort by specific field. Sorting apply in these fields: type, name, first_seen, last_seen.
parameters.description: Select the fields on which to sort the results. Sorting applies in these fields: type, name, first_seen, last_seen. Append :asc or :desc to the key to sort by ascending or descending order respectively. Example: name:desc.

parameters.name: page
parameters.title: Page
parameters.tooltip: Navigate between pages. Defaults to 1.
parameters.description: Specify a page number to retrieve information from that page.

parameters.name: size
parameters.title: Size
parameters.placeholder: Must be greater 0 and less than or equal to 500.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the number of records to fetch per page. Default value is 10 and you can specify a maximum value of 500 per page.
parameters.description: Specify the number of records to fetch per page. Default value is 10 and the maximum value is 500. Value must be greater 0 and less than or equal to 500.

parameters.name: get_all_records
parameters.title: Get All Records
parameters.tooltip: (Optional) Select to retrieve all records. Selecting this parameter ignores values in parameters Page and Size. By default, this is selected, i.e. set to true.
parameters.description: Select this parameter to ignore the values specified in the Page and Size parameters. By default, this parameter is selected, i.e. set to true.

operations.title: Get Specific Adversary Centric Intelligence ACI IOCs
operations.description: Retrieves specific IOCs for the organization ID specified in the configuration parameters published by Fortinet FortiRecon ACI, based on the provided IOC ID.

parameters.name: ioc_id
parameters.title: IOC ID
parameters.description: Specify the ACI Intel IOC ID to retrieve details about the specified IOC from FortiRecon ACI.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the ACI Intel IOC id to get the Ioc details.

parameters.name: task_id
parameters.title: Task ID
parameters.description: Specify the task ID to update the status.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the task ID to update the status.

parameters.title: Status
parameters.name: status
parameters.tooltip: Specify the status to update the Task.
parameters.description: Specify the status to update the task. You can choose from pending, failed, completed.
